articles.title: Can the World’s Economic Firefighter Adapt to the 21st Century?
articles.description: As wars, trade spats, and AI reshape the global economy, the IMF has held its role as the world’s lender-of-last-resort steady. Much of its governance, including countries’ shares of voting rights, have remained the same over decades, often requiring lending countries to return to the same austerity playbook. Gita Gopinath, former first managing director of the IMF says that the stability is by design in times of greater geopolitical uncertainty while Boston University’s Kevin Gallagher says that some parts of the IMF are due for an upgrade. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Discovery of a novel vulnerability in aggressive lymphoma could change future therapy
articles.description: A research team at the University of Cologne's Center for Molecular Medicine Cologne (CMMC) has discovered that the protein cFLIP can be used to override the defenses of Diffuse Large B Cell Lymphoma (DLBCL) against programmed cell death. These defenses often cause treatments to be ineffective because they allow the cancer cells to survive. The results are especially relevant to ABC-DLBCL, a specific subtype of blood cancer that has poor patient prognosis and survival outcome. The new study appeared under the title "Expression of cFLIP in B cells is essential for diffuse large B cell lymphoma pathogenesis" in the Blood Journal.

articles.title: Smell loss may mark Alzheimer's start as olfactory damage map comes into focus
articles.description: A research team has, for the first time, identified at the cellular level why the olfactory system is the first to be damaged in the early stages of Alzheimer's disease (dementia). The paper is published in the journal Alzheimer's & Dementia. The team was led by Prof. Moon Cheil of the Department of Brain Sciences at the Daegu Gyeongbuk Institute of Science and Technology, in collaboration with a team led by Prof. Ali Jahanshahi at Maastricht University in the Netherlands.

articles.title: The plan to quietly kill Coyote v. Acme blew up in David Zaslav’s face
articles.description:
This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on Hollywood trends and streaming culture, follow Charles Pulliam-Moore. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes on Sundays at 8AM ET. Opt in for The Stepback here.
How it started
Under David Zaslav's leadership, WBD got very into the practice of shelving its own nearly completed projects in order to cash in on subsequent tax write-offs. To help deal with its looming debt and operating costs, the studio killed Adil El Arbi and Bilall Fallah's live-action Batgirl feature and the Scoob! Holiday Haunt movie from Michael Kurinsky  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Blood vessels found in T. rex bones are rewriting dinosaur science
articles.description: Dinosaur DNA may still be out of reach, but scientists are uncovering something almost as exciting—ancient blood vessels hidden inside fossilized bones. In a massive Tyrannosaurus rex nicknamed Scotty, researchers discovered a network of preserved vessels within a rib that once fractured and began healing 66 million years ago. Using powerful synchrotron X-rays from particle accelerators, they were able to peer inside the dense fossil without damaging it, revealing intricate, iron-rich structures left behind by the healing process.

articles.title: DNA research just rewrote the origin of human species
articles.description: Scientists have uncovered a surprising new picture of human origins that challenges the long-held idea of a single ancestral population in Africa. By analyzing genetic data from diverse modern African groups—especially the highly distinct Nama people—and comparing it with fossil evidence, researchers found that early humans likely evolved from multiple intermingling populations over hundreds of thousands of years. Rather than a clean split, these groups stayed connected, exchanging genes even after beginning to diverge around 120,000–135,000 years ago.

articles.title: Fish oil may be hurting your brain, new study finds
articles.description: Fish oil has long been praised as brain-boosting, but new research suggests the story may be more complicated. Scientists found that in people with repeated mild head injuries, a key omega-3 fatty acid in fish oil—EPA—may actually interfere with the brain’s ability to repair itself. Instead of helping recovery, it appears to weaken blood vessel stability, disrupt healing signals, and even contribute to harmful protein buildup linked to cognitive decline.

articles.title: Wild's overtime win was overshadowed by Stars' defenseman getting cut by a skate

articles.title: Can the World’s Economic Firefighter Adapt to the 21st Century?
articles.description: As wars, trade spats, and AI reshape the global economy, the IMF has held its role as the world’s lender-of-last-resort steady. Much of its governance, including countries’ shares of voting rights, have remained the same over decades, often requiring lending countries to return to the same austerity playbook. Gita Gopinath, former first managing director of the IMF says that the stability is by design in times of greater geopolitical uncertainty while Boston University’s Kevin Gallagher says that some parts of the IMF are due for an upgrade. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Kadapa city under siege for twenty years, decries MLA Madhavi Reddy
articles.description: Government whip and Kadapa MLA Reddeppagari Madhavi Reddy said the city had remained underdeveloped for 20 years, blaming earlier civic administrations, after flagging off a Census awareness rally

dailyNews.title: Colombia hosts talks on exiting fossil fuels as global energy crisis deepens
dailyNews.description: A gathering in Colombia reflects mounting impatience with the slow pace of global climate negotiations. Countries first agreed at COP28 in Dubai in 2023 to "transition away from fossil fuels," but made little progress on it.

dailyNews.title: Northern Ireland homes evacuated after reports of car bomb explosion
dailyNews.description: Police in Northern Ireland declared a security alert in the town of Dunmurry, on the outskirts of Belfast, after reports that a car bomb exploded near a police station.

dailyNews.title: "I Want To Go Home": Erica Kirk In Tears After White House Dinner Shooting
dailyNews.description: The moment has captured an attention especially after her husband, Charlie Kirk, an ally of Trump, was shot dead in September last year while speaking at Utah Valley University.

dailyNews.title: What we know about the suspected ‘assassination attempt’ on Donald Trump
dailyNews.description: A man armed is in custody after trying to rush the White House Correspondents' Dinner, which Trump was attending.

dailyNews.title: HORMUZ TRACKER: Traffic Halted With Blockades Firmly in Place
dailyNews.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has remained at a near-complete halt, with neither Iran nor the US showing any sign of easing their blockades of maritime traffic.

dailyNews.title: Trump cancels Kushner, Witkoff trip to Pakistan for Iran talks
dailyNews.description: U.S. envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner were expected to head to Islamabad Saturday, but President Trump said later that his "representatives" would not be going.

dailyNews.title: White House shooting details emerge: Suspect ‘assembled long weapon in makeshift room’ before opening fire
dailyNews.description: White House shooting: Interim Washington, DC, police chief Jeffery Carroll reportedly said that the suspect in the shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner had several weapons as he “charged” a Secret Service checkpoint.

dailyNews.title: Iranian Negotiators Set to Return to Pakistan to Try to Revive Truce Talks
dailyNews.description: It was not clear whether President Trump would send his negotiators for a new round of discussions after abruptly calling off a trip by his top advisers on Saturday.

dailyNews.title: Islamabad Reopens After U.S.-Iran Talks Fail to Materialize
dailyNews.description: Officials had locked the city down, anticipating talks between U.S. and Iranian delegations. But they didn’t happen. “What did I close my business for?” one business owner asked.

dailyNews.title: King Charles U.S. visit comes at tense moment in transatlantic relationship
dailyNews.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive Monday for a four day U.S. state visit. Some hope the royal touch can heal the transatlantic rift that's emerged under Trump.

dailyNews.title: Sexual misconduct scandals in Washington spark scramble for reforms, expedited investigations
dailyNews.description: Scandals and resignations have reignited a debate about whether Washington's rules and institutions for self-oversight can keep pace with the unfolding misconduct.

dailyNews.title: Barabak: The congressional landmine stirring fears about the midterm election — and a Trump power grab
dailyNews.description: A constitutional provision allowing Congress to determine its makeup has some worried a Democratic victory could be nullified. Skepticism abounds, but Trump has repeatedly breached long-standing norms.

dailyNews.title: National parks brace for summer surge as Trump administration proposes more staff cuts
dailyNews.description: The push to cut the park system even further has underscored ongoing questions about how smoothly parks can operate as warm weather and summer vacations draw tourists.

dailyNews.title: U.S. Sanctions Zigzag in New World of Economic Warfare
dailyNews.description: With oil prices in mind, the Trump administration has deployed a haphazard approach to sanctions on Russia and Iran.

dailyNews.title: Trump, allies use dinner shooting to press case for White House ballroom
dailyNews.description: A White House ballroom would be far more secure than any hotel facility, Trump says, as he and his allies argue that Saturday’s shooting bolsters their case.

dailyNews.title: What we know about the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting
dailyNews.description: An armed man exchanged gunfire with law enforcement after he rushed a security checkpoint at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in Washington, D.C. on Saturday, authorities said, an event attended by President Donald Trump and high-ranking members of his administration.

dailyNews.title: Trump safe after shots fired at White House Correspondents' Dinner; suspect in custody
dailyNews.description: President Trump was safely evacuated from the White House Correspondents' Dinner Saturday night after shots were fired outside the ballroom of the Washington Hilton Hotel.

dailyNews.title: WHCA dinner shooting live updates: Suspect armed with multiple guns and knives
dailyNews.description: A suspect is in custody following a shooting incident Saturday at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner at the Washington, D.C. Hilton hotel.

dailyNews.title: Suspect in White House Correspondents' Association dinner shooting identified
dailyNews.description: The man suspected of opening fire at officers during the White House Correspondents' Association dinner in Washington, D.C., on Saturday is a 31-year-old from Southern California, authorities said

dailyNews.title: Trump: Shooter at White House press dinner had multiple weapons, one officer shot
dailyNews.description: Trump, seated next to first lady Melania Trump, was quickly surrounded by Secret Service agents, while many attendees ducked under their tables.

dailyNews.title: ‘He has the market in a chokehold’: Stocks swing as Trump posts
dailyNews.description: US stock market moves over the past 15 months have been heavily influenced by President Donald Trump’s remarks and social media posts. Fundstrat analysis shows his comments drove the S&P 500’s biggest gains and losses, an influence unmatched by any modern US leader. Analysts say this level of market impact is unprecedented and highlights investors’ sensitivity to political signals.

dailyNews.title: Trump says "it's a dangerous profession" after shots fired at dinner
dailyNews.description: President Trump was evacuated from the White House Correspondents' Dinner after shots rang out at the hotel where the event was taking place.

dailyNews.title: Live updates: Officials identify suspect in White House correspondents’ dinner shooting
dailyNews.description: The suspect will be charged with three counts and will be arraigned Monday, U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro said.

dailyNews.title: Trump Describes His Mind-Set After Shooting at White House Correspondents’ Dinner
dailyNews.description: Very little was clear about what had happened at the Washington Hilton on Saturday night. But the president wanted to talk about it.
